content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

March 26, 1985


David B. Rosenthal
Attorney at Law
145 Park Place
Point Richmond, CA  94801

                                          Re:  Your Request for Advice on
                                               Behalf of James McMillan
                                               Our File No. A-85-056

Dear Mr. Rosenthal:

     This letter will confirm my previous telephone advice to
you regarding Mr. James McMillan's committee and the candidacy
of Mr. Ahmad Anderson.  The facts are as follows:

                                    FACTS

     Mr. McMillan is presently an elected member of the Richmond
City Council.  He was elected two years ago and his term does
not expire for another two years; consequently, he is not an
active candidate in the current Richmond City elections.
However, as an incumbent he is considered a "candidate" for
purposes of the Political Reform Act1/ and he has a campaign
committee.  You have stated:

          Mr. McMillan has an existing committee which is
     presently being used solely for the purpose of
     receiving contributions and making expenditures on
     behalf of Mr. Ahmad Anderson, a candidate running for
     City Council in the May election.

          Mr. Anderson has no control or involvement over
     how the funds raised by the McMillan Committee are

_________________________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
specified.  For a definition of "candidate" see Section 82007.

David B. Rosenthal
March 26, 1985
Page 2


spent, nor has he any influence on the monies being
raised.

     Mr. Anderson does not have a committee of his own
nor is he raising any money or making any expenditures
on his own behalf. All funds being expended on behalf
of Mr. Anderson's candidacy for the City Council of
Richmond are being raised, spent and fully controlled
by the McMillan committee.

     You have further advised me that Mr. Anderson is really a
"puppet" candidate. Given the foregoing unique set of facts,
our advice is as follows:

                              ANALYSIS

     Mr. McMillan's committee is making independent expenditures
on Mr. Anderson's behalf and must report them on a Form 465,
Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report, pursuant to the
election filing schedule for the May election. Mr. McMillan's
committee will also have to disclose this activity on its Form
490 filings pursuant to the standard filing schedule contained
on Page 6 of the enclosed Campaign Manual. For specific
assistance as to when statements are required to be filed, you
should consult the enclosed Campaign Manual or contact the City
Clerk's Office or our Political Reform Consultant Mary Ann
Kvasager, at (916) 322-5662.

                              CAVEAT

     As I indicated previously, this advice depends on the
accuracy of your description of the factual situation. I bring
this to your attention because it is extremely unusual for any
candidate for public office to run no campaign on his own behalf
and simply to allow someone else to do it for him, without any
input at all. If Mr. Anderson has any control or input over the
expenditures, including what the content of any advertisements,
mailings, brochures, fliers, etc., etc., will be, if the
expenditures are made with the cooperation, consultation, or
suggestion of Mr. Anderson, or in coordination or concert with
Mr. Anderson, then Mr. McMillan's committee is not making
independent expenditures -- but is making in-kind contributions
to Mr. Anderson (which Mr. Anderson must report).2/ (See our
regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215, copy enclosed.)

_________________
     2/ See our previous letter to James McMillan, dated
February 13, 1985, copy enclosed.

David B. Rosenthal
March 26, 1985
Page 3


     If Mr. Anderson has received in-kind contributions from
Mr. McMillan's committee, Mr. Anderson will have to file pre-and
post-election candidate campaign statements on dates specified
for the May election, list the total amount spent on his behalf
by Mr. McMillan's committee on Schedule C and itemize all
monetary or in-kind contributors of $100 or more.  Again, you
may wish to consult with the City Clerk's Office or with our
Technical Assistance and Analysis staff.

                              Sincerely,


                              Robert E. Leidigh
                              Counsel
                              Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosure
cc:  Mr. Hayden, Richmond City Clerk

Low Offices of David B. Rosenthal
Attorneys at Law

MAR 4 2:48 FM 05

March 1, 1985

A 85-256

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Attn: Mary Ann Kvasater

Dear Ms. Kvasater:

This office represents Mr. James McMillan, who is presently
an elected City Council member of the City of Richmond.
Mr. McMillan is not running for re-election at the present
time. Mr. McMillan has an existing committee which is presently
being used solely for the purpose of receiving contributions
and making expenditures on behalf of Mr. Ahmad Anderson, a
candidate running for City Council in the May election.

Mr. Anderson has no control or involvement over how the funds
raised by the McMillan Committee are spent, nor has he any
influence on the monies being raised.

Mr. Anderson does not have a committee of his own nor is he
raising any money or making any expenditures on his own behalf.
All funds being expended on behalf of Mr. Anderson's candidacy
for the City Council of Richmond are being raised, spent and
fully controlled by the McMillan committee.

Please advise what reporting obligations the McMillan committee
has and what, if any, reporting obligations Mr. Anderson has.

Please respond to this office. Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

DAVID B. ROSENTHAL

DBR:c

cc: James McMillan

145 Park Place, Point Richmond, California 94801 (415) 232-4700

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441

April 22, 1985


David B. Rosenthal
145 Park Place
Point Richmond, CA  94801

                                        Re:  Your Letter of April 10, 1985
                                             Regarding Our Letter of
                                             March 26, 1985
                                             Our File No. A-85-056

Dear Mr. Rosenthal:

     In your letter of April 10, 1985, you disagreed with the
statement in my Advice Letter of March 26, 1985, that
Mr. Anderson was a "puppet" candidate. Although in our
telephone conversation you used that word to describe
Mr. Anderson's candidacy, you now have told me that either you
did not intend the word "puppet" to be taken literally or that,
if you did, it was a mistake. You have inquired whether our
advice depended on your use of the word "puppet," in reference
to Mr. Anderson's candidacy.

     The advice letter addressed the entire situation you
described -- that of a candidate who has nothing whatever to do
with raising contributions or making expenditures for his own
campaign. However you characterize this situation, the advice
remains the same. Nevertheless, since you have stated that
Mr. Anderson was not merely a "puppet" candidate, I would
strongly advise you to review very carefully the "Caveat"
portion of my March 26, 1985, advice letter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Robert B. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:plh

Law Offices of David B. Rosenthal
Attorneys at Law

APR 17 6 37AM 85

April 10, 1985


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

I am in receipt of your letter of March 26, 1985. You have
misstated and misquoted the information that I have given you.
You indicate in the second full paragraph that I advised you
that Mr. Anderson is really a "puppet" candidate. This infor-
mation is inaccurate and I have never advised you of such a
fact. To the extent that your analysis is dependent upon the
characterization of Mr. Anderson's candidacy as a "puppet"
candidate, you are advised to reconsider and revise your
advice.

Please advise me forthwith whether your analysis and inter-
pretation of the requirements for Mr. McMillan and Mr. Anderson
are different based on an accurate assessment of the facts as
set forth in the information prior to the paragraph where you
have misstated my representations.

Sincerely,

DAVID B. ROSENTHAL

DBR:gs
cc: Mr. Hayden
    Richmond City Clerk


145 Park Place, Point Richmond, California 94801 (415) 232-4700

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting obligations for James McMillan's campaign committee, which is raising and spending funds solely on behalf of another candidate (Ahmad Anderson) who has no control over or involvement in the committee's activities?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: McMillan's committee must report independent expenditures on Form 465 and Form 490; however, if Anderson has any control, input, or coordination with the expenditures, they would be in-kind contributions requiring Anderson to file campaign statements and report McMillan's committee as a contributor.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (March 1, 1985) and FPPC advice letter response (March 26, 1985), plus follow-up correspondence (April 10 and April 22, 1985) clarifying the characterization of Anderson as a 'puppet' candidate. Extracted from the primary advice letter dated March 26, 1985.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Please advise what reporting obligations the McMillan committee has and what, if any, reporting obligations Mr. Anderson has.

CONCLUSION: Mr. McMillan's committee is making independent expenditures on Mr. Anderson's behalf and must report them on a Form 465, Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report, pursuant to the election filing schedule for the May election. Mr. McMillan's committee will also have to disclose this activity on its Form 490 filings pursuant to the standard filing schedule.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that McMillan's committee making expenditures for Anderson's campaign must file Form 465 independent expenditure reports and Form 490 campaign statements, with a caveat that if Anderson has any involvement or control over the expenditures, they would constitute in-kind contributions requiring different reporting by Anderson.
